measured_cold_start.source: inference-lab/evidence/i3/logs/llama-server-{calib,direct,gw,failover-1,failover-2}.log + attempt1-cancel/llama-server-cancel.log (warm regime, OS page cache holds the weights); inference-lab/evidence/i3/logs/llama-server-cancel.log (final) + llama-server-sharedprefix.log (cold regime, page cache evicted); llama.cpp commit 8f114a9, qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf, same 4-vCPU CPU-only host throughout

real_measured_capacity_scenario.basis: measured (mock backend, loop-mechanics labeling per FL-T004)
real_measured_capacity_scenario.finding: no headroom deficit: N-1 capacity (33.16 rps) already exceeds the bursty workload's peak (20 rps) -- losing one of two replicas is not a cold-start risk for this specific (measured mock capacity, this workload) pairing.

illustrative_assumed_capacity_scenario.basis: ASSUMED (illustrative only, chosen below the bursty peak to demonstrate the mechanism -- NOT a measured capacity)
illustrative_assumed_capacity_scenario.finding: warm vs cold replacement changes the cold-start window by 8.5x (11.9s vs 101.3s) with steady-state capacity and offered load held fixed -- the resulting backlog and drain time scale by the same 8.5x (59.7 vs 506.7 requests; 6.0s vs 50.7s to drain) -- direct support for planning-prompt hypothesis 3: cold-start headroom is set by warm-up time x deficit rate, not steady-state throughput.
